findings.title: GitHub Actions workflow detected
findings.description: A GitHub Actions workflow file was found.
findings.recommendation: Pin actions by SHA, set least-privilege permissions, and avoid long-lived secrets.

findings.title: Unpinned GitHub Actions detected
findings.description: Floating tags/branches can increase supply-chain risk.
findings.recommendation: Pin third-party actions to a full commit SHA.

findings.title: Unpinned third-party GitHub Action
findings.description: Matched rule from PipeScope rule pack.
findings.recommendation: Pin all third-party GitHub Actions to a full commit SHA to reduce supply-chain risk.

findings.title: Weak hardcoded secret value detected
findings.description: A secret-like configuration key is assigned a weak/default value ('secret'), which is insecure.
findings.recommendation: Replace weak/default secrets with strong random values and load them from a secret manager or protected environment variables. Never hardcode secrets in code.

findings.title: Weak hardcoded secret value detected
findings.description: A secret-like configuration key is assigned a weak/default value ('changeme'), which is insecure.
findings.recommendation: Replace weak/default secrets with strong random values and load them from a secret manager or protected environment variables. Never hardcode secrets in code.

findings.title: Weak hardcoded secret value detected
findings.description: A secret-like configuration key is assigned a weak/default value ('1234'), which is insecure.
findings.recommendation: Replace weak/default secrets with strong random values and load them from a secret manager or protected environment variables. Never hardcode secrets in code.

findings.title: Weak hardcoded secret value detected
findings.description: A secret-like configuration key is assigned a weak/default value ('secret'), which is insecure.
findings.recommendation: Replace weak/default secrets with strong random values and load them from a secret manager or protected environment variables. Never hardcode secrets in code.

findings.title: Weak default secret value
findings.description: Matched rule PS-SECRET-WEAK-001 (CWE-798)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded secret and rotate affected credentials.

findings.title: Hardcoded database password
findings.description: Matched rule PS-SECRET-DB-001 (CWE-798)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded secret and rotate affected credentials.

findings.title: Hardcoded database password
findings.description: Matched rule PS-SECRET-DB-001 (CWE-798)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded secret and rotate affected credentials.

findings.title: Base image may match known CVEs
findings.description: Image 'ubuntu:20.04' heuristically matched 5 CVE(s) based on product/version search.
findings.recommendation: Validate with an image-level scanner such as Trivy or Grype, then upgrade to a fixed image version or digest.

findings.title: Container may run as root
findings.description: Running as root increases blast radius if the container is compromised.
findings.recommendation: Create and switch to a non-root user (USER appuser) and drop Linux capabilities where possible.

findings.title: COPY . . without .dockerignore
findings.description: Copying the whole repo without .dockerignore can bake secrets and unnecessary files into the image.
findings.recommendation: Add a .dockerignore excluding secrets (.env, *.pem, *.key), VCS (.git), and build artifacts.

findings.title: Container may run as root
findings.description: Running as root increases blast radius if the container is compromised.
findings.recommendation: Create and switch to a non-root user (USER appuser) and drop Linux capabilities where possible.

findings.title: Missing .dockerignore while copying build context
findings.description: A Dockerfile appears to copy the repository context (e.g., COPY . ...), but no .dockerignore file was found. This can bake secrets and unnecessary files into container images.
findings.recommendation: Add a .dockerignore excluding secrets (.env, *.pem, *.key), VCS (.git), and build artifacts (node_modules, dist, build).

findings.title: Potential Jenkins CVE: CVE-2011-4344
findings.description: A potential vulnerability (CVE-2011-4344) was found for Jenkins version 2.516.1. Please investigate further.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Jenkins to the latest version and review the CVE details at https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2011-4344

findings.title: Exposed Jenkins Endpoint: login
findings.description: A potentially sensitive Jenkins endpoint was found at http://localhost:8080/login
findings.recommendation: Ensure that only authorized users have access to this endpoint. Review your Jenkins security configuration.
